rationale: Both correctly identify the duplicate-of-#4808 disposition, but X asserts unverified specifics as fact (PR 'merged', fabricated grep/comment output, fabricated 'Verified end-to-end' claim in the reproduction) and only tacks on a disclaimer that contradicts its confident tone. Y reaches the same core verdict but is explicit about what it could and couldn't verify (flagging #4808 as closed-without-merging and a test-coverage gap in _STALE_API_FILES as open questions rather than settled facts), which better matches the rubric's preference for well-argued hedging over confident overreach. Y also grounds its answer in more specific, plausible file/line references and more fully serves the reporter's actual need (confirming whether the fix is truly on main).
